2603.21036.title: Left Behind: Cross-Lingual Transfer as a Bridge for Low-Resource Languages in Large Language Models
2603.21036.paper_summary: We investigate how large language models perform on low-resource languages by benchmarking eight LLMs across five experimental conditions in English, Kazakh, and Mongolian. Using 50 hand-crafted questions spanning factual, reasoning, technical, and culturally grounded categories, we evaluate 2,000 responses on accuracy, fluency, and completeness. We find a consistent performance gap of 13.8-16.7 percentage points between English and low-resource language conditions, with models maintaining surface-level fluency while producing significantly less accurate content. Cross-lingual transfer-prompting models to reason in English before translating back-yields selective gains for bilingual architectures (+2.2pp to +4.3pp) but provides no benefit to English-dominant models. Our results demonstrate that current LLMs systematically underserve low-resource language communities, and that effective mitigation strategies are architecture-dependent rather than universal.
2603.21036.paper_summary_zh: 我們調查大型語言模型在低資源語言上的表現，通過在英語、哈薩克語和蒙古語中對八個LLM進行五種實驗條件的基準測試。使用50個手工製作的問題，涵蓋事實、推理、技術和文化根基類別，我們評估了2,000個回應的準確性、流暢性和完整性。我們發現英語和低資源語言條件之間存在13.8-16.7個百分點的一致表現差距，模型在保持表面流暢性的同時，產生的內容準確性顯著較低。跨語言轉移提示模型在翻譯回之前先用英語推理，對雙語架構帶來選擇性增益（+2.2pp至+4.3pp），但對英語主導模型則沒有任何好處。我們的結果表明，目前的LLM系統性地未能滿足低資源語言社群的需求，而有效的緩解策略依賴於架構，而非普遍適用。
2603.21036.author: Abdul-Salem Beibitkhan et.al.
